name: omnata-plugin-devkit
documentation:
# omnata-plugin-devkit
Tools for client side development, testing and uploading of [Omnata Plugins](https://docs.omnata.com/omnata-product-documentation/omnata-sync-for-snowflake/plugins).


For instructions on creating plugins, visit our [docs site](https://docs.omnata.com/omnata-product-documentation/omnata-sync-for-snowflake/plugins/creating-plugins).

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.path
documentation: Returns itself in pathlib.Path format

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.chmod
documentation: Change the file mode and permissions, like os.chmod().

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.parent
documentation: The logical parent of the path. For details, check pathlib.Path.parent

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.absolute
documentation: Make the path absolute, without normalization or resolving symlinks.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.iterdir
documentation:
When the path points to a directory, yield path objects of the directory contents.
Otherwise, NotADirectoryError is raised.
If the location does not exist, FileNotFoundError is raised.

For details, check pathlib.Path.iterdir()

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.exists
documentation: Return True if the path points to an existing file or directory.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.is_dir
documentation:
Return True if the path points to a directory (or a symbolic link pointing to a directory),
False if it points to another kind of file.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.is_file
documentation:
Return True if the path points to a regular file (or a symbolic link pointing to a regular file),
False if it points to another kind of file.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.name
documentation: A string representing the final path component.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.restrict_permissions
documentation: Restrict file/directory permissions to owner-only.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.touch
documentation: Create a file at this given path. For details, check pathlib.Path.touch()

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.mkdir
documentation: Create a directory at this given path. For details, check pathlib.Path.mkdir()

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.read_text
documentation:
Return the decoded contents of the file as a string.
Raises an error of the file exceeds the specified size limit.
For details, check pathlib.Path.read_text()

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.write_text
documentation: Open the file pointed to in text mode, write data to it, and close the file.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.open
documentation:
Open the file pointed by this path and return a file object, as
the built-in open() function does.
If the file is opened for reading, [read_file_limit_kb] parameter must be provided.
Raises error if the read file exceeds the specified size limit.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.move
documentation:
Recursively move a file or directory (src) to another location and return the destination.

If dst is an existing directory or a symlink to a directory, then src is moved inside that directory.
The destination path in that directory must not already exist.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.copy
documentation:
Copy the file/directory into the destination.
If source is a directory, its whole content is copied recursively.
Permissions of the copy are limited only to the owner.

If destination is an existing directory, the copy will be created inside it,
unless dirs_exist_ok is true and the destination has the same name as this path.

Otherwise, the copied file/base directory will be renamed to match destination.
If dirs_exist_ok is false (the default) and dst already exists,
a FileExistsError is raised. If dirs_exist_ok is true,
the copying operation will continue if it encounters existing directories,
and files within the destination tree will be overwritten by corresponding
files from the src tree.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.unlink
documentation:
Remove this file or symbolic link.
If the path points to a directory, use SecurePath.rmdir() instead.

Check pathlib.Path.unlink() for details.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.rmdir
documentation:
Remove this directory.
If the path points to a file, use SecurePath.unlink() instead.

If path points to a file, NotADirectoryError will be raised.
If directory does not exist, FileNotFoundError will be raised unless [missing_ok] is True.
If the directory is not empty, DirectoryNotEmpty will be raised unless [recursive] is True.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.secure_path.SecurePath.temporary_directory
documentation:
Creates a temporary directory in the most secure manner possible.
The directory is readable, writable, and searchable only by the creating user ID.
Yields SecurePath pointing to the absolute location of created directory.

Works similarly to tempfile.TemporaryDirectory

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.config.config_init
documentation:
Initializes the app configuration. Config provided via cli flag takes precedence.
If config file does not exist we create an empty one.

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.api.config.get_config_value
documentation: Looks for given key under nested path in toml file.

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.package_utils.parse_requirements
documentation:
Reads and parses a python requirements.txt file.

Args:
    requirements_file (str, optional): The name of the file.
    Defaults to 'requirements.txt'.

Returns:
    list[str]: A flat list of package names, without versions

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.package_utils.deduplicate_and_sort_reqs
documentation: Deduplicates a list of requirements, keeping the first occurrence of each package.

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.package_utils.parse_anaconda_packages
documentation:
Checks if a list of packages are available in the Snowflake Anaconda channel.
Returns a dict with two keys: 'snowflake' and 'other'.
Each key contains a list of Requirement object.

Parameters:
    packages (List[Requirement]) - list of requirements to be checked

Returns:
    result (SplitRequirements) - required packages split to those avaiable in conda, and others, that need to be
                                 installed using pip

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.package_utils.get_downloaded_packages
documentation:
Returns a dict of official package names mapped to the files/folders
that belong to it under the .packages directory.

Returns:
    dict[str:List[str]]: a dict of package folder names to package name

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.package_utils.get_package_name_from_metadata
documentation:
Loads a METADATA file from the dist-info directory of an installed
Python package, finds the name of the package.
This is found on a line containing "Name: my_package".

Args:
    metadata_file_path (str): The path to the METADATA file

Returns:
    str: the name of the package.

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.package_utils.install_packages
documentation:
Install packages from a requirements.txt file or a single package name,
into a local folder named '.packages'.
If a requirements.txt file is provided, they will be installed using pip.
If a package name is provided, it will be installed using pip.
Returns a tuple of:
1) a boolean indicating whether the installation was successful
2) a SplitRequirements object containing any installed dependencies
which are available on the Snowflake anaconda channel. These will have
been deleted from the local packages folder.

classes.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.models.SplitRequirements
documentation:
A dataclass to hold the results of parsing requirements files and dividing them into
snowflake-supported vs other packages.

classes.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.models.RequirementWithFiles
documentation:
A dataclass to hold a requirement and the path to the
downloaded files/folders that belong to it

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.snowcli.cli.plugins.snowpark.zipper.add_file_to_existing_zip
documentation:
Adds another file to an existing zip file

Args:
    zip_file (str): The existing zip file
    file (str): The new file to add

modules.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin
documentation: This module contains the implementation of the Omnata Plugin for TemplateApp.

classes.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.__init__
documentation: The plugin class for TemplateApp

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.connection_form
documentation:
Returns one or more ConnectionMethod objects, each of which define a form for
the user to complete during the connection process.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.connect
documentation:
Takes the parameters that the user entered in the connection form and tests the connection.
Alternatively, the ConnectResponse can contain OAuth parameters which will initiate an OAuth flow.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.outbound_configuration_form
documentation: Generates a configuration form which gathers the information needed to sync data out of Snowflake.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.field_mappings
documentation:
This is a handler which populates the field mapping options based on the target object selected.
Each FormOption returned here will be available as a field mapping target in the UI.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.sync_outbound
documentation:
Applies a set of changed records to an app. This function is called whenever a run occurs and changed records
are found.
To return results, either:
\- return one big dataframe at the end
\- invoke outbound_sync_request.enqueue_results() during the load process
\- return a dataframe from a managed_outbound_processing() function

\:param PluginConfigurationParameters parameters the parameters of the sync, as configured by the user
\:param OutboundSyncRequest outbound_sync_request an object describing what has changed
\:return None
\:raises ValueError: if issues were encountered during connection

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.create_records
documentation:
This function is called in parallel with fixed sized batches of records.
This makes it simpler to work with APIs which have mini-bulk endpoints (e.g. 100 records at a time).
splits input dataframe into batches with no more than 100 records each,
invoked in parallel with a maximum of 2 concurrent calls

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.inbound_configuration_form
documentation: Generates a configuration form which gathers the information needed to sync data in to Snowflake.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.inbound_stream_list
documentation:
This method is invoked after the user has configured the inbound sync, and returns a list of streams
that the user can choose from to sync data from. Any configuration parameters from the form above
can be accessed via the parameters object.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_template.plugin.TemplateApp.sync_inbound
documentation:
Retrieves the next set of records from an application.
The inbound_sync_request contains the list of streams to be synchronized.
To return results, invoke inbound_sync_request.enqueue_results() during the load process.

\:param PluginConfigurationParameters parameters the parameters of the sync, as configured by the user
\:param InboundSyncRequest inbound_sync_request an object describing what needs to be sync'd
\:return None
\:raises ValueError: if issues were encountered during connection
Omnata Plugin for TemplateApp

This file should be copied into the root of the Airbyte connector, adjacent to the main.py and requirements.txt files.

modules.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session
documentation: A companion for developing Omnata Plugins, including generation of test cases with application request mocking.

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.scrub_secrets
documentation: A secret scrubber for pyvcr, to redact the parts of the HTTP request which came from connection secret values

classes.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.SyncScenario
documentation: Represents a scenario where we sync some data to an app, while capturing records and traffic

classes.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.fetch_record_state
documentation: Collects the full set of records which were previously staged

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.prepare_outbound_scenario
documentation:
Prepares an outbound sync scenario using entirely client-side generated information.
The input dataframe must be in the standard outbound record stare format, and
all parameters must be provided, including connection parameters and secrets,
sync parameters and field mappings.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.prepare_outbound_scenario_from_sync
documentation:
Prepares an outbound sync scenario using a Sync which has been fully configured in the Omnata app.
The user must have the Omnata application role PLUGIN_DEVELOPER, as well as having develop privileges
on the plugin app.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.prepare_inbound_scenario_from_sync
documentation:
Prepares an outbound sync scenario using a Sync which has been fully configured in the Omnata app.
The user must have the Omnata application role PLUGIN_DEVELOPER, as well as having develop privileges
on the plugin app.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.cancel_scenario
documentation: Cancels the current scenario, and stops recording traffic.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.complete_scenario
documentation: Marks the scenario as complete, and stops recording traffic.

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession._stop_recording
documentation: Stops the pyvcr recording

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession._start_recording
documentation: Configures and starts the pyvcr recording

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession._restart_recording
documentation: Stops and starts the recording

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.validate_response
documentation:
Tests that the response to a record apply request is valid. This will check that the DataFrame
has all of the correct columns, and that results of all the originally requested records are included

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.dataframe_to_behave_table
documentation: Converts a pandas DataFrame into a formatted Behave table

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.development_session.DevelopmentSession.generate_behave_test
documentation: Generates a Python Behave test, following completed test scenarios
Connects to Snowflake and manages test case related concerns

modules.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_uploader
documentation:
Eventually, this will create an application in Snowflake representing an Omnata Plugin, and registers it.
Requires that the Omnata engine app is already installed.

Since Application Objects don't exist yet, currently we create a database and do all the registration stuff ourselves.
In future, it'll create an application capable of registering itself upon install/upgrade.

Deliberately avoids having the user create a python package for their code, since it shouldn't be necessary effort.
Instead we just upload all .py files adjacent to the file containing the plugin class.

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_uploader.prepare_version_comment
documentation: The manifest file takes a comment, but since it's in YAML you have to do a bunch of silly work to make it valid and multiline.

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_uploader.find_omnata_plugin_in_module
documentation: Searches within a module for subclasses of OmnataPlugin

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_uploader.function_signature
documentation:
Provides a few different variations of the function signature for use in SQL.
The initial parameter is the connection object, which we may want to remove or replace with a connection slug.
When outputing the function signature, we can include just the names, just the data types, or both.

functions.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_uploader.udf_wrapper_definition
documentation: Creates a wrapper UDF which allows the UDF/UDTF to be invoked with the connection slug instead of the connection object

classes.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_uploader.PluginUploader

methods.name: omnata_plugin_devkit.plugin_uploader.PluginUploader.upload_plugin
documentation:
Creates a stage and uploads the latest plugin artifacts, Omnata runtime etc.
If the schema name is not provided, the plugin_id from the manifest will be used.
The schema name is returned so that upstream processes know where the stage was created.

Creates a setup script which will create the plugin UDFs:
1. OMNATA_PLUGIN_INFO() - returns the plugin manifest
2. UPDATE_API_CONFIGURATION() - Used by Omnata to advise the plugin of any new external access integration
objects and secret objects. These are stored in the OMNATA_REGISTRATION table.
2. CONFIGURE_APIS() - Used by Omnata to advise the plugin of any new external access integration
objects and secret objects. These are stored in the OMNATA_REGISTRATION table.

The script will also create the stored procs which receive requests from Omnata.
It uses the information in the OMNATA_REGISTRATION table (if any) to include the necessary
external access integrations or secrets bindings.
After plugin installation, the user will grant the plugin's OMNATA_MANAGEMENT role to the Omnata app.
Then it will become visible to Omnata, and Omnata will create its own application role for the plugin.
Then the user will grant that application role to the plugin app, and trust will go both ways.
At this point, the plugin can be registered from the Omnata side.
Any time there are new secrets or external access integrations, Omnata will call CONFIGURE_APIS to update
all the procs.
Uploads plugins to a Snowflake account and registers them with the Omnata app.
